evidence.title: README (segment 1)
README (segment 2)
evidence.excerpt: This repository houses all Bitwarden client applications except the mobile applications ([iOS](https://github.com/bitwarden/ios) | [android](https://github.com/bitwarden/android)).

evidence.title: README (segment 3)
evidence.excerpt: Please refer to the [Clients section](https://contributing.bitwarden.com/getting-started/clients/) of the [Contributing Documentation](https://contributing.bitwarden.com/) for build instructions, recommended tooling, code style tips, and lots of other great information to get you started.

evidence.title: README (segment 4)
evidence.excerpt: - [bitwarden/server](https://github.com/bitwarden/server): The core infrastructure backend (API, database, Docker, etc). - [bitwarden/ios](https://github.com/bitwarden/ios): Bitwarden iOS Password Manager & Authenticator apps. - [bitwarden/android](https://github.com/bitwarden/android): Bitwarden Android Password Manager & Authenticator apps. - [bitwarden/directory-connector](https://github.com/bitwarden/directory-connector): A tool for syncing a directory (AD, LDAP, Azure, G Suite, Okta) to an organization.

evidence.title: README (segment 5)
evidence.excerpt: Interested in contributing in a big way? Consider joining our team! We're hiring for many positions. Please take a look at our [Careers page](https://bitwarden.com/careers/) to see what opportunities are [currently open](https://bitwarden.com/careers/#open-positions) as well as what it's like to work at Bitwarden.

evidence.title: README (segment 6)
evidence.excerpt: Code contributions are welcome! Please commit any pull requests against the `main` branch. Learn more about how to contribute by reading the [Contributing Guidelines](https://contributing.bitwarden.com/contributing/). Check out the [Contributing Documentation](https://contributing.bitwarden.com/) for how to get started with your first contribution.

evidence.title: README (segment 7)
evidence.excerpt: Security audits and feedback are welcome. Please open an issue or email us privately if the report is sensitive in nature. You can read our security policy in the [`SECURITY.md`](SECURITY.md) file.

evidence.title: Desktop 2026.2.1 lack the `login in with device`
evidence.excerpt: Desktop 2026.2.1 lack the `login in with device` (state:closed)

evidence.title: web-v2026.2.1
evidence.excerpt: * Added email protected Sends * Added background upgrade of KDF settings for accounts below allowed minimums * Added prompt to upgrade KDF settings for accounts below allowed minimums * Updated loading state UI for access intelligence * Updated UI for individual subscription page * Improved individual subscription page handling of edge cases * Core cryptography improvements to master password salt * Various under-the-hood improvements and minor bug fixes

evidence.title: desktop-v2026.2.1
evidence.excerpt: - Added email protected Sends - Added background upgrade of KDF settings for accounts below allowed minimums - Added prompt to upgrade KDF settings for accounts below allowed minimums - Updated Send UI - Core cryptography improvements to master password salt - Various under-the-hood improvements and minor bug fixes Thank you! 💙 A big shout-out to the following community members for their contributions! [lzl0304](https://github.com/lzl0304) - Fix lock vault from system tray not working

evidence.title: cli-v2026.2.0
evidence.excerpt: - Added email protected Sends - Bug-fix for confirm user command - Various under-the-hood improvements and minor bug fixes Thank you! 💙 A big shout-out to the following community members for their contributions!
